paragraphs.summary: This supervisory statement (SS) sets out the expectations of the Prudential Regulation Authority (PRA) on PRA-authorised firms in respect of the PRA rules on contractual stays in financial contracts g
This statement is intended to be read together with the Stay in Resolution Part of the CRR Firms and Non-Authorised Persons Sectors of the PRA Rulebook.

This statement is relevant to PRA-authorised banks, building societies, PRA-designated investment firms and their qualifying parent undertakings, which for this purpose comprise UK financial holding c

This statement is also relevant to credit institutions, investment firms and financial institutions that are subsidiaries of those firms listed in paragraph 1.3, regardless of the jurisdiction of esta
This statement is also relevant to counterparties of UK firms and in-scope subsidiaries with third-country law financial arrangements.

By setting out the PRA’s expectations with regard to certain aspects of the rules, this statement may help to minimise the adverse effect that the failure of a PRA firm could have on financial stabili
The PRA considers the following examples to be a non-exhaustive list of non-material amendments for purposes of these rules:

The prohibition in the rules applies unless the counterparty has agreed in an enforceable manner that its termination rights and rights to enforce a security interest will be limited in accordance wit

Legal opinions are not required by the rules as a matter of course. However, the PRA expects UK firms to satisfy themselves that they (and relevant subsidiaries) are in compliance with the rules and U
Breaches of the rules will be dealt with in accordance with standard PRA policy.

As noted in CP19/151, the EU Bank Recovery and Resolution Directive (BRRD)2 empowers competent authorities and resolution authorities to require a firm to maintain detailed records of financial contra
